event: The Reception of Henry III of France in Venice
symbols: classical colonnade — Venetian civic grandeur and the Republic's self-presentation as heir to Roman dignity
red banner — the standard of Venice or the royal colors of ceremony
parasol held over the king — royal honor and the ceremonial reception of a sovereign guest
harbor and ships in the background — Venice as a maritime power and gateway between East and West
dense crowd of nobles and attendants — the full pageantry of state diplomacy and alliance-making
description: A wide horizontal fresco depicting a grand ceremonial reception set within a classical architectural space with tall Corinthian columns framing the scene on both sides. At the center-left, a richly dressed figure — likely Henry III — stands beneath a parasol held by an attendant, surrounded by nobles in elaborate costume. Opposite him, another dignitary in ceremonial robes receives the visitor. A large red banner or flag rises above the crowd. Dozens of figures in Renaissance-era clothing fill the columned space, with gestures of greeting, conversation, and pageantry.
